# System Prompt: bowling-kata

You are working on **bowling-kata**, a polyglot monorepo implementing Uncle Bob's Bowling Game Kata in four languages (Java, Python, TypeScript, Go), exposing bowling scoring logic via REST APIs and a Go CLI, with a shared leaderboard service and Docker Compose orchestration.

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Primary API | Java 21, Spring Boot, Maven |
| Python API | Python, FastAPI, pytest |
| TypeScript Service | Node.js, Express, Jest |
| Go CLI | Go, `pkg/bowling`, `cmd/scorer` |
| Testing | JUnit 5, pytest, Jest, go test |
| Infra | Docker Compose, Redis, Prometheus |

---

## Commands

```bash
# Install
mvn dependency:resolve && pip install -r api/requirements.txt && cd services/scoring-service && npm ci

# Build
mvn package

# Run (Java API)
mvn spring-boot:run

# Test
mvn test                          # Java
pytest api/                       # Python (run from repo root)
cd services/scoring-service && npx jest  # TypeScript
go test ./pkg/bowling/...         # Go
```

---

---

## Domain Concepts

- **Bowling / Game**: Core scoring class (Java `Bowling`, Go `Game`) with `roll(pins)` and `score()` methods. Uses a 21-element rolls array to handle up to 10 frames including the 10th-frame bonus rolls.
- **Frame**: One unit of play (1–2 rolls, up to 3 in the 10th frame). Classified as **Strike**, **Spare**, or open.
- **Strike**: All 10 pins on roll 1; bonus = next 2 rolls' value.
- **Spare**: All 10 pins in 2 rolls; bonus = next 1 roll's value.
- **PlayerScore**: Domain object holding player name, score, and timestamp; sorts highest-first.
- **LeaderboardService**: Thread-safe, in-memory Spring service. Scores are **not** persisted — lost on restart.
- **GameController**: Spring REST controller. `POST /api/score` → calculate + record; `GET /api/leaderboard` → top-10.
- **ScoringRequest / ScoringResponse**: Java DTOs for the `/api/score` endpoint (name + rolls in; name + score + rank out).
- **calculateScore** (TypeScript): Computes total score, frame breakdown, and perfect-game flag from a rolls array.

---

## Architecture & Conventions

- **Structure**: Monorepo with four independent language implementations of the same scoring algorithm. Changes to scoring logic **must be kept consistent across all four** (`Bowling.java`, `api/main.py`, `pkg/bowling/game.go`, `services/scoring-service/src/index.ts`).
- **Naming**: camelCase throughout all languages.
- **Source roots**: `src/main/java/bowling` (Java), `api/` (Python), `pkg/bowling` + `cmd/scorer` (Go), `services/scoring-service/src` (TypeScript).
- **Go imports**: Always use the full module path `github.com/vb-nattamai/bowling-kata`, never relative imports.
- **Python tests**: Must run from repo root (`pytest api/`) — `api/` must be importable as a package.
- **10th frame**: Allows up to 3 rolls; the 21-element array is intentional — off-by-one errors here are a common bug.
- **Tools directory**: Contains unresolved `{{PLACEHOLDER}}` scaffolds. Do not modify or execute anything in `tools/`.

---

## Key Components

| Component | Path | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `Bowling` | `src/main/java/bowling/Bowling.java` | Java scoring logic |
| `BowlingApplication` | `src/main/java/bowling/BowlingApplication.java` | Spring Boot entry point |
| `GameController` | `src/main/java/bowling/api/GameController.java` | REST endpoints |
| `LeaderboardService` | `src/main/java/bowling/service/LeaderboardService.java` | In-memory leaderboard |
| `PlayerScore` | `src/main/java/bowling/domain/PlayerScore.java` | Score domain object |
| `FastAPI main` | `api/main.py` | Python scoring API |
| `scoring-service` | `services/scoring-service/src/index.ts` | TypeScript scoring API |
| `Go game` | `pkg/bowling/game.go` | Go scoring logic |
| `Go CLI` | `cmd/scorer/main.go` | Go CLI scorer |
